sections.paragraph:
ἐπείγω 1 aor. pass. ἠπείχθην Jer 17:16 Sym; Hos 6:3 Quinta (Joseph.) (in var. mgs. since Hom.; LXX; TestJos 2:2; Joseph.; Just., A I, 46, 6) to exert pressure against, press hard τὸ ἐν ἐμοὶ ἐπείγει με προελθεῖν what is in me presses hard to come forth = my baby is about to come GJs 17:3.—DELG.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

var. var. = various(ly)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 360
